name: Vegetable Spring Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1 oz Cellophane noodles (soaked in warm water for 20 min, drained and chopped fine)
3 small Potatoes (peeled and cut paper thin with a peeler and then shredded)
1 tbsp Tree ears (soaked in warm water for 20 min, drained and chopped)
1 medium Carrot (peeled, cut into 1 inch sections and shredded)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all ingredients except rice papers and oil.
2. Cut the rice papers into quarters. Wet the surface of each paper with water using your fingers or a brush; within about 1 minute, the paper will become flexible enough to be filled.
3. Place about 1 tablespoon of the filling on each quartered paper and roll up. Fold the unrolled ends inward to seal.
4. Heat the oil in a wok to about 350°F (175°C). Cook the rolls for 20 minutes (10 minutes each side) until golden brown and crispy.
5. Serve with Buddhist Nuoc Leo.
Note: An alternative form of spring roll skin is available, which does not require deep frying. This wrapper is inherently sticky and stretchy, white and barely opaque. Lightly sauteing the carrots and potatoes beforehand is an option. Fresh Chinese parsley or cilantro can also be included in this version.
